title: VEA ETF Review: Vanguard Developed Markets (2026)
description: Complete review of Vanguard VEA ETF. Expense ratio, holdings, performance, and comparison vs IEFA and SPDW. Best for international stock diversification.

mainEntity.name: What is the VEA ETF expense ratio in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Vanguard FTSE Developed Markets ETF (VEA) has an expense ratio of 0.05%.

mainEntity.name: Is VEA a good ETF for international exposure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, VEA is one of the most popular and cost-effective ETFs for gaining broad exposure to developed markets outside of the US, holding roughly 4,000 stocks across Europe, Asia, and Canada.

mainEntity.name: How does VEA compare to IEFA?

acceptedAnswer.text: VEA (Vanguard) and IEFA (iShares) are highly comparable developed market ETFs. VEA has an expense ratio of 0.05%, while IEFA charges 0.07%. VEA includes Canada and South Korea, while IEFA (tracking MSCI EAFE) generally excludes them.

mainEntity.name: What are the top holdings in VEA?

acceptedAnswer.text: Top holdings typically include massive non-US multinationals like ASML, Samsung, Novo Nordisk, Nestle, and Toyota.
Yes, VEA pays a quarterly dividend. Its trailing 12-month yield usually hovers around 2.5% to 3.5%, depending on global market conditions and currency fluctuations.
